# Local Recipe Planner - Your Custom Meal Plan

## Suggested Recipes

### 1. Mapo Tofu (麻婆豆腐)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Silken tofu (400g)
- Minced beef (150g)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (10g)
- Pixian Doubanjiang (2 tbsp)
- Sichuan Peppercorn Powder (1 tsp)
- Fermented Black Beans (1 tbsp)
- Chili Oil (2 tbsp)
- Light Soy Sauce (1 tbsp)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tbsp)
- Cornstarch (1 tbsp)
- Green Onion (2 stalks)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the silken tofu into 2cm cubes and blanch in hot, lightly salted water for 1 minute to firm up, then drain gently.
2. Finely mince the garlic and ginger. Finely chop the fermented black beans and Pixian doubanjiang.
3. Heat cooking oil in a wok over medium heat. Add the minced beef and fry until crispy and browned, then set aside or push to the side of the wok.
4. Add the chopped Pixian doubanjiang to the oil and fry until the oil turns red and fragrant. Add the minced ginger, garlic, and fermented black beans, stirring to combine.
5. Pour in the Shaoxing rice wine and light soy sauce, then add the water or broth and bring to a simmer.
6. Gently slide in the blanched tofu cubes. Simmer on low heat for 5 minutes, allowing the tofu to absorb the flavors.
7. Thicken the sauce by drizzling in the cornstarch slurry (cornstarch mixed with water) in 2-3 stages, gently stirring with the back of a ladle to avoid breaking the tofu.
8. Drizzle with chili oil and sprinkle with Sichuan peppercorn powder and chopped green onions before serving.

**Missing Ingredients:** Pixian Doubanjiang, Sichuan Peppercorn Powder, Fermented Black Beans, Chili Oil, Light Soy Sauce, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Cornstarch, Green Onion

----------------------------------------

### 2. Sichuan Dan Dan Noodles (担担面)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Minced beef (150g)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (10g)
- Sui Mi Ya Cai (50g)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tbsp)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tbsp)
- Chinese Sesame Paste (2 tbsp)
- Chili Oil (3 tbsp)
- Sichuan Peppercorn Powder (1 tsp)
- Chicken Broth (200ml)
- Wheat Noodles (200g)
- Green Onion (2 stalks)

**Instructions:**
1. Finely mince the ginger and garlic.
2. Heat a tablespoon of oil in a wok over medium heat. Add the minced beef and fry until crisp. Splash in Shaoxing rice wine, light soy sauce, and add the Sui Mi Ya Cai. Fry until dry and fragrant, then set the meat topping aside.
3. In serving bowls, prepare the noodle sauce base: whisk together Chinese sesame paste, chili oil, light soy sauce, minced garlic, and Sichuan peppercorn powder with a ladle of hot chicken broth.
4. Cook the wheat noodles in boiling water according to package instructions. Drain well.
5. Divide the noodles into the serving bowls over the sauce base.
6. Top with the fried minced beef and Sui Mi Ya Cai mixture, and garnish with chopped green onions.

**Missing Ingredients:** Sui Mi Ya Cai, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Light Soy Sauce, Chinese Sesame Paste, Chili Oil, Sichuan Peppercorn Powder, Wheat Noodles, Green Onion

----------------------------------------

### 3. Sichuan Spicy Beef and Tofu Stew (麻辣豆腐煲)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Silken tofu (400g)
- Minced beef (100g)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (10g)
- Pixian Doubanjiang (1.5 tbsp)
- Chili Powder (1 tbsp)
- Sichuan Peppercorns (1 tsp)
- Light Soy Sauce (1 tbsp)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tbsp)
- Cornstarch (1 tbsp)
- Chicken Broth (300ml)
- Coriander (10g)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the silken tofu into large cubes. Mince the garlic and ginger.
2. Heat oil in a claypot or deep pan over medium-high heat. Add the minced beef and stir-fry until fully cooked and slightly browned.
3. Add Pixian doubanjiang, minced ginger, garlic, and whole Sichuan peppercorns, frying until the oil is red and aromatic.
4. Add the chili powder, light soy sauce, and Shaoxing rice wine, then pour in the chicken broth. Bring to a boil.
5. Gently add the silken tofu cubes. Lower heat to a simmer, cover, and cook for 10 minutes to let the flavours penetrate the tofu.
6. Slowly stir in the cornstarch slurry to slightly thicken the broth.
7. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve piping hot.

**Missing Ingredients:** Pixian Doubanjiang, Chili Powder, Sichuan Peppercorns, Light Soy Sauce, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Cornstarch, Coriander

# Local Recipe Planner - Your Custom Meal Plan

## Suggested Recipes

### 1. Authentic Gong Bao Ji Ding (Kung Pao Chicken)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Chicken Breasts (300g)
- Spring Onions (4 stalks)
- Dried Red Chilies (10 pieces)
- Sichuan Peppercorns (1 tsp)
- Roasted Peanuts (50g)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (15g)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tsp)
- Dark Soy Sauce (1/2 tsp)
- Shaoxing Wine (1 tsp)
- Chinkiang Vinegar (1 tbsp)
- Sugar (1 tbsp)
- Cornstarch (2 tsp)
- Cooking Oil (2 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the chicken breast into 1.5cm cubes. Marinate with 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1 tsp Shaoxing wine, and 1 tsp cornstarch for 15 minutes.
2. Prepare the sauce by mixing 1 tbsp Chinkiang vinegar, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1/2 tsp dark soy sauce, 1 tsp cornstarch, and 1 tbsp water or stock in a small bowl.
3. Slice the spring onions into 1.5cm sections, keeping the white and green parts separated. Mince the garlic and ginger.
4. Heat 2 tbsp of cooking oil in a wok over medium-low heat. Add Sichuan peppercorns and dried red chilies, stirring until fragrant and slightly dark (do not burn).
5. Turn heat to high, add the marinated chicken and stir-fry until it turns white.
6. Add the minced garlic, ginger, and the white parts of the spring onions. Stir-fry for 1 minute.
7. Pour in the sauce mixture and stir quickly to coat. The sauce should thicken immediately.
8. Toss in the roasted peanuts and the green parts of the spring onions, stir to combine, and serve hot.

**Missing Ingredients:** Dried Red Chilies, Sichuan Peppercorns, Roasted Peanuts, Garlic, Ginger, Light Soy Sauce, Dark Soy Sauce, Shaoxing Wine, Chinkiang Vinegar, Sugar, Cornstarch

----------------------------------------

### 2. Sichuan Scallion Chicken (Cong You Ji)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Chicken Breasts (300g)
- Spring Onions (6 stalks)
- Ginger (20g)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tbsp)
- Sugar (1/2 tsp)
- Shaoxing Wine (1 tbsp)
- Sesame Oil (1 tsp)
- Cooking Oil (3 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Poach the chicken breast in water with a slice of ginger and 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine until cooked through (about 12-15 minutes). Cool and shred.
2. Finely chop the spring onions and place them in a heatproof bowl with minced ginger.
3. Heat 3 tbsp of cooking oil in a small pan until smoking hot, then pour it over the spring onions and ginger to release their aroma.
4. Add light soy sauce, sugar, and sesame oil to the scallion oil mixture and stir well.
5. Pour the aromatic scallion sauce over the shredded chicken and toss well before serving.

**Missing Ingredients:** Ginger, Light Soy Sauce, Sugar, Shaoxing Wine, Sesame Oil

----------------------------------------

### 3. Lizhi Ji Ding (Lychee-Flavoured Chicken)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Chicken Breasts (300g)
- Spring Onions (4 stalks)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (15g)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tsp)
- Chinkiang Vinegar (1.5 tbsp)
- Sugar (1.5 tbsp)
- Shaoxing Wine (1 tsp)
- Cornstarch (2 tsp)
- Cooking Oil (2 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the chicken breast into 1.5cm cubes. Marinate with 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1 tsp Shaoxing wine, and 1 tsp cornstarch.
2. Mix the sweet and sour sauce: 1.5 tbsp Chinkiang vinegar, 1.5 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1/2 tsp salt, 1 tsp cornstarch, and 2 tbsp chicken stock.
3. Cut the spring onions into short rounds. Mince the garlic and ginger.
4. Heat oil in a wok. Stir-fry the chicken cubes on high heat until cooked through, then remove and set aside.
5. In the remaining oil, stir-fry the garlic, ginger, and spring onions until fragrant.
6. Return the chicken to the wok, pour in the sauce mixture, and stir quickly until the sauce thickens and glazes the chicken.

**Missing Ingredients:** Garlic, Ginger, Light Soy Sauce, Chinkiang Vinegar, Sugar, Shaoxing Wine, Cornstarch

# Local Recipe Planner - Your Custom Meal Plan

## Suggested Recipes

### 1. Authentic Gong Bao Ji Ding (Kung Pao Chicken)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Chicken Breasts (300g)
- Spring Onions (4 stalks)
- Dried Red Chilies (10 pieces)
- Sichuan Peppercorns (1 tsp)
- Roasted Peanuts (50g)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (15g)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tsp)
- Dark Soy Sauce (1/2 tsp)
- Shaoxing Wine (1 tsp)
- Chinkiang Vinegar (1 tbsp)
- Sugar (1 tbsp)
- Cornstarch (2 tsp)
- Cooking Oil (2 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the chicken breast into 1.5cm cubes. Marinate with 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1 tsp Shaoxing wine, and 1 tsp cornstarch for 15 minutes.
2. Prepare the sauce by mixing 1 tbsp Chinkiang vinegar, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1/2 tsp dark soy sauce, 1 tsp cornstarch, and 1 tbsp water or stock in a small bowl.
3. Slice the spring onions into 1.5cm sections, keeping the white and green parts separated. Mince the garlic and ginger.
4. Heat 2 tbsp of cooking oil in a wok over medium-low heat. Add Sichuan peppercorns and dried red chilies, stirring until fragrant and slightly dark (do not burn).
5. Turn heat to high, add the marinated chicken and stir-fry until it turns white.
6. Add the minced garlic, ginger, and the white parts of the spring onions. Stir-fry for 1 minute.
7. Pour in the sauce mixture and stir quickly to coat. The sauce should thicken immediately.
8. Toss in the roasted peanuts and the green parts of the spring onions, stir to combine, and serve hot.

**Missing Ingredients:** Dried Red Chilies, Sichuan Peppercorns, Roasted Peanuts, Garlic, Ginger, Light Soy Sauce, Dark Soy Sauce, Shaoxing Wine, Chinkiang Vinegar, Sugar, Cornstarch

----------------------------------------

### 2. Sichuan Scallion Chicken (Cong You Ji)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Chicken Breasts (300g)
- Spring Onions (6 stalks)
- Ginger (20g)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tbsp)
- Sugar (1/2 tsp)
- Shaoxing Wine (1 tbsp)
- Sesame Oil (1 tsp)
- Cooking Oil (3 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Poach the chicken breast in water with a slice of ginger and 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine until cooked through (about 12-15 minutes). Cool and shred.
2. Finely chop the spring onions and place them in a heatproof bowl with minced ginger.
3. Heat 3 tbsp of cooking oil in a small pan until smoking hot, then pour it over the spring onions and ginger to release their aroma.
4. Add light soy sauce, sugar, and sesame oil to the scallion oil mixture and stir well.
5. Pour the aromatic scallion sauce over the shredded chicken and toss well before serving.

**Missing Ingredients:** Ginger, Light Soy Sauce, Sugar, Shaoxing Wine, Sesame Oil

----------------------------------------

### 3. Lizhi Ji Ding (Lychee-Flavoured Chicken)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Chicken Breasts (300g)
- Spring Onions (4 stalks)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (15g)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tsp)
- Chinkiang Vinegar (1.5 tbsp)
- Sugar (1.5 tbsp)
- Shaoxing Wine (1 tsp)
- Cornstarch (2 tsp)
- Cooking Oil (2 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the chicken breast into 1.5cm cubes. Marinate with 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1 tsp Shaoxing wine, and 1 tsp cornstarch.
2. Mix the sweet and sour sauce: 1.5 tbsp Chinkiang vinegar, 1.5 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1/2 tsp salt, 1 tsp cornstarch, and 2 tbsp chicken stock.
3. Cut the spring onions into short rounds. Mince the garlic and ginger.
4. Heat oil in a wok. Stir-fry the chicken cubes on high heat until cooked through, then remove and set aside.
5. In the remaining oil, stir-fry the garlic, ginger, and spring onions until fragrant.
6. Return the chicken to the wok, pour in the sauce mixture, and stir quickly until the sauce thickens and glazes the chicken.

**Missing Ingredients:** Garlic, Ginger, Light Soy Sauce, Chinkiang Vinegar, Sugar, Shaoxing Wine, Cornstarch

# Local Recipe Planner - Your Custom Meal Plan

## Suggested Recipes

### 1. Authentic Tomato Scrambled Eggs (番茄炒蛋)

**Required Ingredients:**
- large free range eggs (4)
- cherry tomatoes (300g)
- Scallions (2 stalks)
- Garlic (2 cloves)
- Sugar (1 tsp)
- Salt (0.5 tsp)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tsp)
- Vegetable Oil (2 tbsp)
- Sesame Oil (0.5 tsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Whisk the large free range eggs in a bowl with a pinch of salt and a splash of Shaoxing rice wine.
2. Halve the cherry tomatoes. Finely chop the scallions, separating the white and green parts, and mince the garlic.
3. Heat vegetable oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Pour in the eggs and scramble gently until just set but still slightly runny. Remove from the wok and set aside.
4. Add a little more oil to the wok. Stir-fry the garlic and scallion whites until fragrant.
5. Add the cherry tomatoes to the wok and cook until they soften and release their juices, pressing lightly with your spatula to create a saucy consistency.
6. Season with sugar and salt to taste.
7. Return the scrambled eggs to the wok, gently fold them into the tomato mixture, and drizzle with sesame oil.
8. Garnish with the scallion greens and serve hot as a classic homestyle dish.

**Missing Ingredients:** Scallions, Garlic, Sugar, Salt, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Vegetable Oil, Sesame Oil

----------------------------------------

### 2. Tomato Egg Drop Soup (番茄蛋花汤)

**Required Ingredients:**
- large free range eggs (2)
- cherry tomatoes (150g)
- Ginger (5g)
- Scallions (1 stalk)
- Sesame Oil (0.5 tsp)
- White Pepper Powder (0.25 tsp)
- Salt (1 tsp)
- Cornstarch (1 tbsp)
- Vegetable Oil (1 tsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Halve the cherry tomatoes. Thinly slice the ginger and finely chop the scallions.
2. Mix the cornstarch with two tablespoons of water in a small bowl to make a slurry.
3. Whisk the large free range eggs in a separate bowl.
4. Heat a teaspoon of vegetable oil in a pot. Sauté the ginger and cherry tomatoes until the tomatoes soften and begin to break down.
5. Pour in 750ml of water and bring to a boil. Lower the heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
6. Stir in the cornstarch slurry to lightly thicken the soup base.
7. Turn off the heat (or lower to very low) and slowly drizzle in the beaten eggs in a thin stream while stirring gently in one direction to create beautiful, thin egg ribbons.
8. Season with salt, white pepper powder, and sesame oil. Garnish with chopped scallions and serve immediately.

**Missing Ingredients:** Ginger, Scallions, Sesame Oil, White Pepper Powder, Salt, Cornstarch, Vegetable Oil

----------------------------------------

### 3. Tomato and Egg Gravy Noodles (番茄鸡蛋面)

**Required Ingredients:**
- large free range eggs (3)
- cherry tomatoes (250g)
- Wheat Noodles (200g)
- Garlic (2 cloves)
- Scallions (2 stalks)
- Light Soy Sauce (1 tbsp)
- Sugar (1 tsp)
- Salt (0.5 tsp)
- Vegetable Oil (2 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cook the wheat noodles in boiling water according to package instructions, drain, and distribute into serving bowls.
2. Whisk the large free range eggs with a pinch of salt. Halve the cherry tomatoes, mince the garlic, and chop the scallions.
3. Heat vegetable oil in a wok and scramble the eggs until softly set. Remove from the wok and set aside.
4. Add another tablespoon of oil to the wok and fry the garlic and scallion whites until fragrant.
5. Add the cherry tomatoes and cook until soft and saucy, using a spatula to mash them slightly.
6. Season the tomatoes with light soy sauce, sugar, and salt. Add a splash of water to make a rich gravy and simmer for 2 minutes.
7. Stir in the scrambled eggs and simmer for another minute to let the eggs absorb the rich tomato juices.
8. Ladle the hot tomato and egg gravy over the prepared noodles, garnish with scallion greens, and serve hot.

**Missing Ingredients:** Wheat Noodles, Garlic, Scallions, Light Soy Sauce, Sugar, Salt, Vegetable Oil

----------------------------------------

### 1. Cantonese Steamed Sea Bass Fillets (清蒸鲈鱼片)

**Required Ingredients:**
- sea bass fillets (300g)
- ginger (30g)
- spring onions (3 stalks)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tbsp)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tbsp)
- Sugar (1/2 tsp)
- Vegetable Oil (2 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Arrange ginger slices and a few spring onion sections on a heatproof plate, then place the sea bass fillets on top.
2. Shred the remaining ginger and spring onions finely into matchsticks.
3. Steam the sea bass fillets over high heat for 6-8 minutes until cooked through.
4. Carefully remove the plate from the steamer and discard the steamed ginger, spring onions, and any accumulated fish water.
5. Scatter the fresh shredded ginger and spring onions on top of the steamed fish.
6. Heat vegetable oil in a small saucepan until smoking hot, then carefully pour it over the ginger and spring onions to release their aroma.
7. Mix light soy sauce, Shaoxing rice wine, and sugar, then pour this sauce mixture around the base of the fish and serve immediately.

4. Place the fish fillets on a heatproof plate, spread the black bean mixture evenly over them, and scatter the sliced ginger on top.
5. Steam over high heat for 6-8 minutes.
6. Scatter chopped spring onions over the fish, and drizzle with smoking-hot vegetable oil before serving.

**Required Ingredients:**

- Garlic (4 cloves)
- Rock Sugar (40g)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (3 tbsp)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tbsp)
- Dark Soy Sauce (1.5 tbsp)
- Star Anise (1 piece)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the pork belly into 2cm cubes. Blanch in boiling water with a few slices of ginger and a splash of Shaoxing wine for 5 minutes, then drain and rinse.
2. In a wok over low heat, melt the rock sugar with a little oil until it caramelizes into a light amber liquid.
3. Add the pork belly and stir-coat with the caramel.
4. Add the ginger, garlic, star anise, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and Shaoxing wine. Stir well to color the pork.
5. Pour in enough boiling water to just cover the meat. Bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on low heat for 45-60 minutes until the pork is tender.
6. Uncover, turn the heat up to high, and reduce the sauce until it is thick, glossy, and clings to the pork.

- Pork Belly (500g)
- Ginger (20g)
- Garlic (4 cloves)
- Rock Sugar (30g)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (2 tbsp)
- Star Anise (2 pieces)
- Cinnamon Stick (1 piece)
- Dried Red Chilies (3 pieces)
- Light Soy Sauce (1 tbsp)
- Cooking Oil (1 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the pork belly into 2.5cm cubes. Blanch in boiling water for 5 minutes, then drain.
2. Heat oil in a wok, add rock sugar, and stir constantly over low heat until melted and golden brown.
3. Toss in the pork belly and stir-fry until caramelized.
4. Add the garlic, ginger, star anise, cinnamon stick, and dried red chilies. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.

- Garlic (4 cloves)
- Pixian Broad Bean Paste (1 tbsp)
- Sichuan Peppercorns (1 tsp)
- Star Anise (2 pieces)
- Rock Sugar (15g)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (2 tbsp)
- Dark Soy Sauce (1 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the pork belly into bite-sized cubes. Blanch in boiling water with ginger for 5 minutes, then drain.
2. Heat a wok over medium-low heat, dry-fry the pork belly until it starts releasing its own fat and turns slightly golden, then set aside.
3. In the rendered pork fat, fry the Pixian broad bean paste (Doubanjiang) and rock sugar until the oil turns red.

# Local Recipe Planner - Your Custom Meal Plan

## Suggested Recipes

### 1. Mapo Tofu (麻婆豆腐)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Silken tofu (400g)
- Minced beef (150g)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (10g)
- Pixian Doubanjiang (2 tbsp)
- Sichuan Peppercorn Powder (1 tsp)
- Fermented Black Beans (1 tbsp)
- Chili Oil (2 tbsp)
- Light Soy Sauce (1 tbsp)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tbsp)
- Cornstarch (1 tbsp)
- Green Onion (2 stalks)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the silken tofu into 2cm cubes and blanch in hot, lightly salted water for 1 minute to firm up, then drain gently.
2. Finely mince the garlic and ginger. Finely chop the fermented black beans and Pixian doubanjiang.
3. Heat cooking oil in a wok over medium heat. Add the minced beef and fry until crispy and browned, then set aside or push to the side of the wok.
4. Add the chopped Pixian doubanjiang to the oil and fry until the oil turns red and fragrant. Add the minced ginger, garlic, and fermented black beans, stirring to combine.
5. Pour in the Shaoxing rice wine and light soy sauce, then add the water or broth and bring to a simmer.
6. Gently slide in the blanched tofu cubes. Simmer on low heat for 5 minutes, allowing the tofu to absorb the flavors.
7. Thicken the sauce by drizzling in the cornstarch slurry (cornstarch mixed with water) in 2-3 stages, gently stirring with the back of a ladle to avoid breaking the tofu.
8. Drizzle with chili oil and sprinkle with Sichuan peppercorn powder and chopped green onions before serving.

**Missing Ingredients:** Pixian Doubanjiang, Sichuan Peppercorn Powder, Fermented Black Beans, Chili Oil, Light Soy Sauce, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Cornstarch, Green Onion

----------------------------------------

### 2. Sichuan Dan Dan Noodles (担担面)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Minced beef (150g)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (10g)
- Sui Mi Ya Cai (50g)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tbsp)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tbsp)
- Chinese Sesame Paste (2 tbsp)
- Chili Oil (3 tbsp)
- Sichuan Peppercorn Powder (1 tsp)
- Chicken Broth (200ml)
- Wheat Noodles (200g)
- Green Onion (2 stalks)

**Instructions:**
1. Finely mince the ginger and garlic.
2. Heat a tablespoon of oil in a wok over medium heat. Add the minced beef and fry until crisp. Splash in Shaoxing rice wine, light soy sauce, and add the Sui Mi Ya Cai. Fry until dry and fragrant, then set the meat topping aside.
3. In serving bowls, prepare the noodle sauce base: whisk together Chinese sesame paste, chili oil, light soy sauce, minced garlic, and Sichuan peppercorn powder with a ladle of hot chicken broth.
4. Cook the wheat noodles in boiling water according to package instructions. Drain well.
5. Divide the noodles into the serving bowls over the sauce base.
6. Top with the fried minced beef and Sui Mi Ya Cai mixture, and garnish with chopped green onions.

**Missing Ingredients:** Sui Mi Ya Cai, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Light Soy Sauce, Chinese Sesame Paste, Chili Oil, Sichuan Peppercorn Powder, Wheat Noodles, Green Onion

----------------------------------------

### 3. Sichuan Spicy Beef and Tofu Stew (麻辣豆腐煲)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Silken tofu (400g)
- Minced beef (100g)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (10g)
- Pixian Doubanjiang (1.5 tbsp)
- Chili Powder (1 tbsp)
- Sichuan Peppercorns (1 tsp)
- Light Soy Sauce (1 tbsp)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tbsp)
- Cornstarch (1 tbsp)
- Chicken Broth (300ml)
- Coriander (10g)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the silken tofu into large cubes. Mince the garlic and ginger.
2. Heat oil in a claypot or deep pan over medium-high heat. Add the minced beef and stir-fry until fully cooked and slightly browned.
3. Add Pixian doubanjiang, minced ginger, garlic, and whole Sichuan peppercorns, frying until the oil is red and aromatic.
4. Add the chili powder, light soy sauce, and Shaoxing rice wine, then pour in the chicken broth. Bring to a boil.
5. Gently add the silken tofu cubes. Lower heat to a simmer, cover, and cook for 10 minutes to let the flavours penetrate the tofu.
6. Slowly stir in the cornstarch slurry to slightly thicken the broth.
7. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve piping hot.

**Missing Ingredients:** Pixian Doubanjiang, Chili Powder, Sichuan Peppercorns, Light Soy Sauce, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Cornstarch, Coriander

# Local Recipe Planner - Your Custom Meal Plan

## Suggested Recipes

### 1. Authentic Gong Bao Ji Ding (Kung Pao Chicken)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Chicken Breasts (300g)
- Spring Onions (4 stalks)
- Dried Red Chilies (10 pieces)
- Sichuan Peppercorns (1 tsp)
- Roasted Peanuts (50g)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (15g)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tsp)
- Dark Soy Sauce (1/2 tsp)
- Shaoxing Wine (1 tsp)
- Chinkiang Vinegar (1 tbsp)
- Sugar (1 tbsp)
- Cornstarch (2 tsp)
- Cooking Oil (2 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the chicken breast into 1.5cm cubes. Marinate with 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1 tsp Shaoxing wine, and 1 tsp cornstarch for 15 minutes.
2. Prepare the sauce by mixing 1 tbsp Chinkiang vinegar, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1/2 tsp dark soy sauce, 1 tsp cornstarch, and 1 tbsp water or stock in a small bowl.
3. Slice the spring onions into 1.5cm sections, keeping the white and green parts separated. Mince the garlic and ginger.
4. Heat 2 tbsp of cooking oil in a wok over medium-low heat. Add Sichuan peppercorns and dried red chilies, stirring until fragrant and slightly dark (do not burn).
5. Turn heat to high, add the marinated chicken and stir-fry until it turns white.
6. Add the minced garlic, ginger, and the white parts of the spring onions. Stir-fry for 1 minute.
7. Pour in the sauce mixture and stir quickly to coat. The sauce should thicken immediately.
8. Toss in the roasted peanuts and the green parts of the spring onions, stir to combine, and serve hot.

**Missing Ingredients:** Dried Red Chilies, Sichuan Peppercorns, Roasted Peanuts, Garlic, Ginger, Light Soy Sauce, Dark Soy Sauce, Shaoxing Wine, Chinkiang Vinegar, Sugar, Cornstarch

----------------------------------------

### 2. Sichuan Scallion Chicken (Cong You Ji)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Chicken Breasts (300g)
- Spring Onions (6 stalks)
- Ginger (20g)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tbsp)
- Sugar (1/2 tsp)
- Shaoxing Wine (1 tbsp)
- Sesame Oil (1 tsp)
- Cooking Oil (3 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Poach the chicken breast in water with a slice of ginger and 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine until cooked through (about 12-15 minutes). Cool and shred.
2. Finely chop the spring onions and place them in a heatproof bowl with minced ginger.
3. Heat 3 tbsp of cooking oil in a small pan until smoking hot, then pour it over the spring onions and ginger to release their aroma.
4. Add light soy sauce, sugar, and sesame oil to the scallion oil mixture and stir well.
5. Pour the aromatic scallion sauce over the shredded chicken and toss well before serving.

**Missing Ingredients:** Ginger, Light Soy Sauce, Sugar, Shaoxing Wine, Sesame Oil

----------------------------------------

### 3. Lizhi Ji Ding (Lychee-Flavoured Chicken)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Chicken Breasts (300g)
- Spring Onions (4 stalks)
- Garlic (3 cloves)
- Ginger (15g)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tsp)
- Chinkiang Vinegar (1.5 tbsp)
- Sugar (1.5 tbsp)
- Shaoxing Wine (1 tsp)
- Cornstarch (2 tsp)
- Cooking Oil (2 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the chicken breast into 1.5cm cubes. Marinate with 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1 tsp Shaoxing wine, and 1 tsp cornstarch.
2. Mix the sweet and sour sauce: 1.5 tbsp Chinkiang vinegar, 1.5 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp light soy sauce, 1/2 tsp salt, 1 tsp cornstarch, and 2 tbsp chicken stock.
3. Cut the spring onions into short rounds. Mince the garlic and ginger.
4. Heat oil in a wok. Stir-fry the chicken cubes on high heat until cooked through, then remove and set aside.
5. In the remaining oil, stir-fry the garlic, ginger, and spring onions until fragrant.
6. Return the chicken to the wok, pour in the sauce mixture, and stir quickly until the sauce thickens and glazes the chicken.

**Missing Ingredients:** Garlic, Ginger, Light Soy Sauce, Chinkiang Vinegar, Sugar, Shaoxing Wine, Cornstarch

----------------------------------------

## Missing Ingredients Price Guide (London Supermarket Generic Prices)

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Shaoxing Wine | Shaoxing Rice Wine | Condiments & Sauces | £2.50 | Traditional Chinese cooking wine, neutralizes meat odors and adds depth. |
| Sesame Oil | Sesame Oil | Pantry & Grains | £2.00 | Toasted sesame oil, highly aromatic, staple for Chinese seasoning. |
| Chinkiang Vinegar | Chinkiang Black Vinegar | Condiments & Sauces | £2.20 | Fruity, complex Chinese black vinegar, perfect for dumpling dipping. |
| Light Soy Sauce | Light Soy Sauce | Condiments & Sauces | £1.50 | Savory, salty Chinese light soy sauce for stir-fries and seasoning. |
| Cornstarch | Cornstarch (Cornflour) | Baking & Cooking Extras | £1.00 | Pure white starch, perfect for thickening sauces and marinades. |
| Dark Soy Sauce | Dark Soy Sauce | Condiments & Sauces | £1.60 | Thick, rich Chinese dark soy sauce for deep color and caramel undertones. |
| Ginger | Ginger Root | Vegetables | £1.40 | Fresh spicy ginger root, excellent for stir-fries and teas. |
| Dried Red Chilies | Chili Flakes | Herbs & Spices | £1.10 | Crushed red hot chili pepper flakes. |
| Garlic | Garlic | Vegetables | £0.65 | Whole garlic bulbs, a staple seasoning for countless dishes. |
| Sichuan Peppercorns | Sichuan Peppercorns | Herbs & Spices | £2.50 | Fragrant, numbing Sichuan peppercorns, essential for authentic Chinese dishes. |
| Roasted Peanuts | Peanut Butter (Smooth) | Condiments & Sauces | £1.90 | 100% roasted peanut smooth spread. |
| Sugar | Granulated Sugar | Pantry & Grains | £1.05 | Sweet white granulated sugar, 1kg. |

# Local Recipe Planner - Your Custom Meal Plan

## Suggested Recipes

### 1. Authentic Tomato Scrambled Eggs (番茄炒蛋)

**Required Ingredients:**
- large free range eggs (4)
- cherry tomatoes (300g)
- Scallions (2 stalks)
- Garlic (2 cloves)
- Sugar (1 tsp)
- Salt (0.5 tsp)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tsp)
- Vegetable Oil (2 tbsp)
- Sesame Oil (0.5 tsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Whisk the large free range eggs in a bowl with a pinch of salt and a splash of Shaoxing rice wine.
2. Halve the cherry tomatoes. Finely chop the scallions, separating the white and green parts, and mince the garlic.
3. Heat vegetable oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Pour in the eggs and scramble gently until just set but still slightly runny. Remove from the wok and set aside.
4. Add a little more oil to the wok. Stir-fry the garlic and scallion whites until fragrant.
5. Add the cherry tomatoes to the wok and cook until they soften and release their juices, pressing lightly with your spatula to create a saucy consistency.
6. Season with sugar and salt to taste.
7. Return the scrambled eggs to the wok, gently fold them into the tomato mixture, and drizzle with sesame oil.
8. Garnish with the scallion greens and serve hot as a classic homestyle dish.

**Missing Ingredients:** Scallions, Garlic, Sugar, Salt, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Vegetable Oil, Sesame Oil

----------------------------------------

### 2. Tomato Egg Drop Soup (番茄蛋花汤)

**Required Ingredients:**
- large free range eggs (2)
- cherry tomatoes (150g)
- Ginger (5g)
- Scallions (1 stalk)
- Sesame Oil (0.5 tsp)
- White Pepper Powder (0.25 tsp)
- Salt (1 tsp)
- Cornstarch (1 tbsp)
- Vegetable Oil (1 tsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Halve the cherry tomatoes. Thinly slice the ginger and finely chop the scallions.
2. Mix the cornstarch with two tablespoons of water in a small bowl to make a slurry.
3. Whisk the large free range eggs in a separate bowl.
4. Heat a teaspoon of vegetable oil in a pot. Sauté the ginger and cherry tomatoes until the tomatoes soften and begin to break down.
5. Pour in 750ml of water and bring to a boil. Lower the heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
6. Stir in the cornstarch slurry to lightly thicken the soup base.
7. Turn off the heat (or lower to very low) and slowly drizzle in the beaten eggs in a thin stream while stirring gently in one direction to create beautiful, thin egg ribbons.
8. Season with salt, white pepper powder, and sesame oil. Garnish with chopped scallions and serve immediately.

**Missing Ingredients:** Ginger, Scallions, Sesame Oil, White Pepper Powder, Salt, Cornstarch, Vegetable Oil

----------------------------------------

### 3. Tomato and Egg Gravy Noodles (番茄鸡蛋面)

**Required Ingredients:**
- large free range eggs (3)
- cherry tomatoes (250g)
- Wheat Noodles (200g)
- Garlic (2 cloves)
- Scallions (2 stalks)
- Light Soy Sauce (1 tbsp)
- Sugar (1 tsp)
- Salt (0.5 tsp)
- Vegetable Oil (2 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cook the wheat noodles in boiling water according to package instructions, drain, and distribute into serving bowls.
2. Whisk the large free range eggs with a pinch of salt. Halve the cherry tomatoes, mince the garlic, and chop the scallions.
3. Heat vegetable oil in a wok and scramble the eggs until softly set. Remove from the wok and set aside.
4. Add another tablespoon of oil to the wok and fry the garlic and scallion whites until fragrant.
5. Add the cherry tomatoes and cook until soft and saucy, using a spatula to mash them slightly.
6. Season the tomatoes with light soy sauce, sugar, and salt. Add a splash of water to make a rich gravy and simmer for 2 minutes.
7. Stir in the scrambled eggs and simmer for another minute to let the eggs absorb the rich tomato juices.
8. Ladle the hot tomato and egg gravy over the prepared noodles, garnish with scallion greens, and serve hot.

**Missing Ingredients:** Wheat Noodles, Garlic, Scallions, Light Soy Sauce, Sugar, Salt, Vegetable Oil

----------------------------------------

| Wheat Noodles | Penne Pasta | Pantry & Grains | £0.95 | Dry penne rigate pasta, 100% durum wheat. |
| Light Soy Sauce | Light Soy Sauce | Condiments & Sauces | £1.50 | Savory, salty Chinese light soy sauce for stir-fries and seasoning. |
| Salt | Salt | Herbs & Spices | £0.60 | Fine table salt for everyday seasoning. |
| Cornstarch | Cornstarch (Cornflour) | Baking & Cooking Extras | £1.00 | Pure white starch, perfect for thickening sauces and marinades. |
| Vegetable Oil | Vegetable Oil | Pantry & Grains | £2.50 | Neutral cooking oil, perfect for frying. |
| Ginger | Ginger Root | Vegetables | £1.40 | Fresh spicy ginger root, excellent for stir-fries and teas. |
| Garlic | Garlic | Vegetables | £0.65 | Whole garlic bulbs, a staple seasoning for countless dishes. |
| Scallions | Spring Onions (Scallions) | Vegetables | £0.55 | Fresh green onions, ideal garnish for stir-fries and salads. |
| Shaoxing Rice Wine | Shaoxing Rice Wine | Condiments & Sauces | £2.50 | Traditional Chinese cooking wine, neutralizes meat odors and adds depth. |

# Local Recipe Planner - Your Custom Meal Plan

## Suggested Recipes

### 1. Cantonese Steamed Sea Bass Fillets (清蒸鲈鱼片)

**Required Ingredients:**
- sea bass fillets (300g)
- ginger (30g)
- spring onions (3 stalks)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tbsp)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tbsp)
- Sugar (1/2 tsp)
- Vegetable Oil (2 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Arrange ginger slices and a few spring onion sections on a heatproof plate, then place the sea bass fillets on top.
2. Shred the remaining ginger and spring onions finely into matchsticks.
3. Steam the sea bass fillets over high heat for 6-8 minutes until cooked through.
4. Carefully remove the plate from the steamer and discard the steamed ginger, spring onions, and any accumulated fish water.
5. Scatter the fresh shredded ginger and spring onions on top of the steamed fish.
6. Heat vegetable oil in a small saucepan until smoking hot, then carefully pour it over the ginger and spring onions to release their aroma.
7. Mix light soy sauce, Shaoxing rice wine, and sugar, then pour this sauce mixture around the base of the fish and serve immediately.

**Missing Ingredients:** Light Soy Sauce, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Sugar, Vegetable Oil

----------------------------------------

### 2. Steamed Sea Bass Fillets with Fermented Black Beans (豉汁蒸鲈鱼片)

**Required Ingredients:**
- sea bass fillets (300g)
- ginger (20g)
- spring onions (2 stalks)
- Fermented Black Beans (1 tbsp)
- Garlic (2 cloves)
- Light Soy Sauce (1 tbsp)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tbsp)
- Vegetable Oil (1 tbsp)
- White Pepper Powder (1/4 tsp)
- Sugar (1/2 tsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Finely chop the fermented black beans and garlic cloves. Thinly slice the ginger and spring onions.
2. Lightly marinate the sea bass fillets with Shaoxing rice wine and white pepper powder for 5 minutes.
3. In a small bowl, mix the chopped black beans, garlic, light soy sauce, sugar, and one teaspoon of vegetable oil to form a paste.
4. Place the fish fillets on a heatproof plate, spread the black bean mixture evenly over them, and scatter the sliced ginger on top.
5. Steam over high heat for 6-8 minutes.
6. Scatter chopped spring onions over the fish, and drizzle with smoking-hot vegetable oil before serving.

**Missing Ingredients:** Fermented Black Beans, Garlic, Light Soy Sauce, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Vegetable Oil, White Pepper Powder, Sugar

----------------------------------------

### 3. Steamed Sea Bass Fillets with Silken Tofu (豆腐蒸鱼片)

**Required Ingredients:**
- sea bass fillets (300g)
- ginger (15g)
- spring onions (2 stalks)
- Silken Tofu (300g)
- Light Soy Sauce (1.5 tbsp)
- Sesame Oil (1 tsp)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (1 tsp)
- Vegetable Oil (1 tbsp)
- White Pepper Powder (1/4 tsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the silken tofu into 1cm thick rectangular slices and arrange them in a single layer on a heatproof plate.
2. Cut the sea bass fillets into bite-sized pieces and marinate with Shaoxing rice wine and white pepper powder.
3. Place one piece of fish fillet on top of each tofu slice. Scatter finely sliced ginger on top.
4. Steam over high heat for 7-8 minutes until the fish is cooked through.
5. Carefully drain any excess water that has accumulated on the plate.
6. Scatter finely shredded spring onions on top. Mix the light soy sauce and sesame oil and pour it around the tofu.
7. Heat vegetable oil until smoking hot, then pour it over the spring onions to finish.

**Missing Ingredients:** Silken Tofu, Light Soy Sauce, Sesame Oil, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Vegetable Oil, White Pepper Powder

----------------------------------------

## Missing Ingredients Price Guide (London Supermarket Generic Prices)

| Garlic | Garlic | Vegetables | £0.65 | Whole garlic bulbs, a staple seasoning for countless dishes. |
| Shaoxing Rice Wine | Shaoxing Rice Wine | Condiments & Sauces | £2.50 | Traditional Chinese cooking wine, neutralizes meat odors and adds depth. |

dataset.prompt_display_text: Fridge contains: pork belly, garlic, ginger. Dietary goals: authentic Chinese red braised pork (Hong Shao Rou). City: London. Suggest recipes, list missing minor ingredients, and retrieve their London prices.
dataset.response_display_text:
# Local Recipe Planner - Your Custom Meal Plan

## Suggested Recipes

### 1. Shanghainese Red Braised Pork (Hong Shao Rou)

**Required Ingredients:**
- Pork Belly (500g)
- Ginger (20g)
- Garlic (4 cloves)
- Rock Sugar (40g)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (3 tbsp)
- Light Soy Sauce (2 tbsp)
- Dark Soy Sauce (1.5 tbsp)
- Star Anise (1 piece)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the pork belly into 2cm cubes. Blanch in boiling water with a few slices of ginger and a splash of Shaoxing wine for 5 minutes, then drain and rinse.
2. In a wok over low heat, melt the rock sugar with a little oil until it caramelizes into a light amber liquid.
3. Add the pork belly and stir-coat with the caramel.
4. Add the ginger, garlic, star anise, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and Shaoxing wine. Stir well to color the pork.
5. Pour in enough boiling water to just cover the meat. Bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on low heat for 45-60 minutes until the pork is tender.
6. Uncover, turn the heat up to high, and reduce the sauce until it is thick, glossy, and clings to the pork.

**Missing Ingredients:** Rock Sugar, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Light Soy Sauce, Dark Soy Sauce, Star Anise

----------------------------------------

### 2. Mao's Hunan Red Braised Pork

**Required Ingredients:**
- Pork Belly (500g)
- Ginger (20g)
- Garlic (4 cloves)
- Rock Sugar (30g)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (2 tbsp)
- Star Anise (2 pieces)
- Cinnamon Stick (1 piece)
- Dried Red Chilies (3 pieces)
- Light Soy Sauce (1 tbsp)
- Cooking Oil (1 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the pork belly into 2.5cm cubes. Blanch in boiling water for 5 minutes, then drain.
2. Heat oil in a wok, add rock sugar, and stir constantly over low heat until melted and golden brown.
3. Toss in the pork belly and stir-fry until caramelized.
4. Add the garlic, ginger, star anise, cinnamon stick, and dried red chilies. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
5. Pour in the Shaoxing rice wine, light soy sauce, and boiling water to cover. Cover and simmer on low heat for 50 minutes.
6. Remove the lid and turn the heat up to reduce the liquid to a thick glaze.

**Missing Ingredients:** Rock Sugar, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Star Anise, Cinnamon Stick, Dried Red Chilies, Light Soy Sauce, Cooking Oil

----------------------------------------

### 3. Sichuan Style Red Braised Pork

**Required Ingredients:**
- Pork Belly (500g)
- Ginger (20g)
- Garlic (4 cloves)
- Pixian Broad Bean Paste (1 tbsp)
- Sichuan Peppercorns (1 tsp)
- Star Anise (2 pieces)
- Rock Sugar (15g)
- Shaoxing Rice Wine (2 tbsp)
- Dark Soy Sauce (1 tbsp)

**Instructions:**
1. Cut the pork belly into bite-sized cubes. Blanch in boiling water with ginger for 5 minutes, then drain.
2. Heat a wok over medium-low heat, dry-fry the pork belly until it starts releasing its own fat and turns slightly golden, then set aside.
3. In the rendered pork fat, fry the Pixian broad bean paste (Doubanjiang) and rock sugar until the oil turns red.
4. Add the Sichuan peppercorns, garlic, ginger, and star anise, and fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
5. Return the pork to the wok, add Shaoxing wine, dark soy sauce, and enough hot water to cover.
6. Cover and simmer on low heat for 50 minutes. Uncover and cook on high heat to reduce and thicken the sauce.

**Missing Ingredients:** Pixian Broad Bean Paste, Sichuan Peppercorns, Star Anise, Rock Sugar, Shaoxing Rice Wine, Dark Soy Sauce

----------------------------------------

## Missing Ingredients Price Guide (London Supermarket Generic Prices)

| Missing Item | Matched London Item | Category | Price | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Cinnamon Stick | Cinnamon Sticks | Herbs & Spices | £1.50 | Whole sweet cinnamon bark sticks. |
| Light Soy Sauce | Light Soy Sauce | Condiments & Sauces | £1.50 | Savory, salty Chinese light soy sauce for stir-fries and seasoning. |
| Cooking Oil | Cooking Oil | Pantry & Grains | £2.20 | Refined neutral oil for stir-frying and deep-frying. |
| Pixian Broad Bean Paste | Pixian Doubanjiang (Chili Bean Paste) | Condiments & Sauces | £2.60 | Authentic fermented broad bean paste from Pixian, essential for Mapo Tofu. |
| Dark Soy Sauce | Dark Soy Sauce | Condiments & Sauces | £1.60 | Thick, rich Chinese dark soy sauce for deep color and caramel undertones. |
| Dried Red Chilies | Chili Flakes | Herbs & Spices | £1.10 | Crushed red hot chili pepper flakes. |
| Shaoxing Rice Wine | Shaoxing Rice Wine | Condiments & Sauces | £2.50 | Traditional Chinese cooking wine, neutralizes meat odors and adds depth. |
| Sichuan Peppercorns | Sichuan Peppercorns | Herbs & Spices | £2.50 | Fragrant, numbing Sichuan peppercorns, essential for authentic Chinese dishes. |
| Rock Sugar | Brown Soft Sugar | Pantry & Grains | £1.60 | Moist soft brown sugar, adds caramel flavor. |
| Star Anise | Star Anise | Herbs & Spices | £1.50 | Whole star anise pods, great for slow-cooked Chinese braises. |

**Estimated Total Cost for Missing Ingredients: £18.60**
